title: Pack a Water Bottle
summary: Bring water with you when going out and remember to drink it.
learnerPromise: You will be able to bring water with you when you leave home and remember to drink it throughout the day.
whyItMatters: Staying hydrated helps you feel more alert, prevents headaches, and keeps your body working well. Carrying water makes it easier to drink enough during the day.
realLifeUses: Taking water to school, work, or a short walk
Remembering to sip water during a warm day outside
Avoiding headaches or tiredness from mild dehydration
Saving money by not buying drinks while out
materialsNeeded: A reusable water bottle or a sealed bottle of water
Access to a sink or water fountain to refill
result: A filled water bottle packed and ready to take with you.
youWillLearn: How to choose a water bottle that is easy to carry
How to fill and seal a bottle so it does not leak
How to remind yourself to drink water during the day
How to refill your bottle when you have access to clean water
miniChallenge: Fill a water bottle, put it in your bag, and finish it before the end of the day. Refill it at least once if you can.
steps: Pick a clean water bottle that fits in your bag or hand.
Fill it with clean, drinkable water.
Make sure the lid is closed tightly and test for leaks.
Place the bottle somewhere easy to reach in your bag.
Take a few sips every hour or whenever you notice thirst.
completionCriteria: You filled and carried a water bottle with you.
You drank most or all of it during the day.
You refilled it at least once if clean water was available.
commonProblems: The bottle leaks because the lid was not closed fully
Drinking too little because the bottle is hard to reach in a bag
tips: Keep your bottle near your keys or phone so you remember it.
Choose a bottle with a reliable seal to avoid spills.
Set a quiet reminder on your phone if you often forget to drink.
Refill whenever you pass a sink or fountain, even if it is still partly full.
